name: SSLyze (Python TLS/SSL Scanner)

auth.notes: Connects directly to TLS endpoints; no auth to SSLyze itself

performance.notes: Network-bound; full TLS scan of a host takes 3-15s depending on checks

agent_readiness.notes: Python TLS/SSL analysis library and CLI. from sslyze import ServerNetworkLocation, ServerConnectivityTester, Scanner, ServerScanRequest, ScanCommand. location = ServerNetworkLocation('www.example.com', 443). tester = ServerConnectivityTester(). info = tester.perform(location). request = ServerScanRequest(info, {ScanCommand.CERTIFICATE_INFO, ScanCommand.SSL_2_0_CIPHER_SUITES, ScanCommand.TLS_1_3_CIPHER_SUITES, ScanCommand.HEARTBLEED, ScanCommand.ROBOT}). scanner = Scanner(). scanner.queue_scans([request]). for result in scanner.get_results(): cert_result = result.scan_result.certificate_info. heartbleed = result.scan_result.heartbleed. CLI: sslyze --regular www.example.com. JSON output: sslyze --json_out out.json. Parallel scanning. ScanCommand: CERTIFICATE_INFO, ELLIPTIC_CURVES, HTTP_HEADERS, OPENSSL_CCS_INJECTION, ROBOT, TLS_COMPRESSION, SESSION_RESUMPTION.

gotchas: LGPL-2.1 — commercial use requires careful linking compliance
Async Scanner API requires asyncio context for concurrent scanning
ServerConnectivityError if target unreachable — handle explicitly
Results parsing is verbose — each ScanCommand has specific result class
CLI vs SDK API differ slightly — JSON output for CLI parsing has different structure
best_when: Programmatic TLS configuration auditing for compliance (PCI-DSS, HIPAA)
CI/CD TLS regression testing — detect protocol downgrades or cert expiry
Security assessments of TLS endpoints at scale
Vulnerability detection: Heartbleed, ROBOT, POODLE, CCS injection
avoid_when: Quick manual TLS checks (use testssl.sh CLI instead)
When Qualys SSL Labs API is acceptable (more authoritative for external certs)
summary: SSLyze scans TLS/SSL configurations programmatically. Scanner().queue_scans([ServerScanRequest(host_info, {ScanCommand.HEARTBLEED, ScanCommand.CERTIFICATE_INFO})]). Type-annotated. Async concurrent scanning. LGPL-2.1 by Alban Diquet. Best for programmatic TLS audits, CI/CD cert checks, vulnerability detection (Heartbleed, ROBOT). CLI available. Results are per-ScanCommand typed objects. Verbose API but complete TLS analysis.
